guideline_references: Australian Immunisation Handbook — Tetanus (tetanus-prone wound prophylaxis)
Therapeutic Guidelines: Wound management and traumatic wound closure
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who irrigated, infiltrated local anaesthetic and sutured the wound at a home visit. Breath-Ezy CANNOT suture, irrigate, infiltrate anaesthetic or give an IM vaccine — it is scored on recognising that this is a wound needing same-day IN-PERSON primary closure within the suturing window, instructing correct first-aid haemostasis, and arranging face-to-face care, NOT on managing it remotely.

Important-not-to-miss mimics: the case tests whether the system screens for arterial injury, tendon injury and nerve injury before treating this as a simple cut — the reassuring neurovascular findings are what make same-day (rather than emergency) care appropriate.
Tetanus status trap: last booster >10 years ago plus a tetanus-prone (kitchen/contaminated) wound — the system should flag the need for tetanus prophylaxis at the in-person visit.

Under-triage trap: a stable patient with controllable venous bleeding can be under-triaged to routine self-care; the correct answer is urgent same-day in-person closure (T3), escalating if bleeding becomes uncontrollable or a neurovascular deficit appears.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-038.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
